channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [MANOJJEWEL] Manoj Jewellers FY26: Revenue Up 91.5%, PAT Up 89.4%
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Manoj Jewellers Limited reported strong FY26 results, with total income increasing by 91.5% YoY to ₹11,419.9 lakhs and PAT growing by 89.4% YoY to ₹902.4 lakhs. The company's EBITDA also saw a significant rise of 63.3% YoY to ₹1,365.4 lakhs. The growth was supported by the expansion of the wholesale business and the introduction of two new product lines. For H2 FY26, total income was up 29.3% YoY, and PAT increased by 34.5% YoY. The company is planning to commission a bangle manufacturing unit and launch a new chains product line in FY27.

  • FY26 Total Income grew 91.5% YoY to ₹11,419.9 lakhs.
  • FY26 PAT increased by 89.4% YoY to ₹902.4 lakhs.
  • H2 FY26 EBITDA margin held steady at 12.16% despite rising gold prices.
  • Company launched 'Thanga Kovil' lightweight temple jewellery collection.
  • B2B revenue growth exceeded 111% YoY in Q2 FY26.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Manoj Jewellers Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [ICICIBANK] ICICI Bank: Prudential to Reduce Stake in ICICI Prudential Life
item.description:
🔥 Trending

ICICI Bank announced that Prudential Corporation Holdings Limited, a promoter of ICICI Prudential Life Insurance, will reduce its shareholding to under 10% from the current 21.91% due to Prudential's acquisition of a 75% stake in Bharti Life Insurance. This move is subject to regulatory approvals. ICICI Bank intends to retain its majority shareholding in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance. Prudential has entered into share purchase agreements with Bharti Life Ventures Private Limited, Bharti Life Insurance Company Limited, and 360 One Private Equity Funds to facilitate the transaction.

  • Prudential to acquire 75% stake in Bharti Life Insurance.
  • Regulatory approvals will require Prudential to reduce its stake in ICICI Prudential Life to under 10%.
  • ICICI Bank intends to maintain its majority shareholding in ICICI Prudential Life.
  • Prudential is engaging with regulatory authorities to determine the divestment timeframe.
  • The transaction involves share purchase agreements with Bharti Life and 360 One Private Equity Funds.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ICICI BANK LTD. | Category: Shareholding

item.title: [ASHIKA] Ashika Credit Capital: Final Dividend of ₹0.50 Recommended
item.description:
Ashika Credit Capital Ltd's board approved audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. A final dividend of ₹0.50 per equity share (5%) was recommended, subject to shareholder approval. Ashika Stock Services Limited became a material wholly-owned subsidiary. The board also approved the transfer of the in-principle approval for sponsorship of the proposed Mutual Fund to Ashika Stock Services Limited, pending SEBI approval. Statutory auditors, DHC & Co., resigned due to ineligibility as per RBI guidelines.

  • Audited financial results (standalone and consolidated) for FY26 approved with unmodified opinion.
  • Final dividend of ₹0.50 per share recommended, subject to shareholder approval.
  • Ashika Stock Services Limited becomes a material wholly-owned subsidiary.
  • Transfer of Mutual Fund sponsorship approval to Ashika Stock Services Limited approved, pending SEBI nod.
  • Statutory auditors DHC & Co. resign due to RBI ineligibility guidelines.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ashika Credit Capital Ltd | Category: Dividend

item.title: [ASHIKA] Ashika Credit Capital: Statutory Auditor Resigns Due to RBI Rules
item.description:
Ashika Credit Capital Ltd (ACCL) announced the resignation of its statutory auditors, M/s DHC & Co, Chartered Accountants, effective May 17, 2026. The resignation is due to the firm's ineligibility under RBI guidelines, as ACCL has crossed the threshold limit of Total Asset Size as on March 31, 2026. The auditors confirmed there are no concerns regarding the company's management or material reasons for their departure beyond the RBI's regulations. The Board also approved the audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026, and recommended a final dividend of ₹0.50 per equity share.

  • M/s DHC & Co resigns as statutory auditor of Ashika Credit Capital due to RBI ineligibility.
  • Board approves audited financial results for FY26 with an unmodified opinion.
  • Final dividend of ₹0.50 per share recommended for FY26, subject to shareholder approval.
  • Ashika Stock Services Limited becomes a material wholly-owned subsidiary.
  • In-principle approval for Mutual Fund sponsorship to be transferred to Ashika Stock Services Limited.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ashika Credit Capital Ltd | Category: Auditor

item.title: [ASHIKA] Ashika Credit Capital Recommends ₹0.50 Dividend
item.description:
Ashika Credit Capital Ltd's board has recommended a final dividend of ₹0.50 per equity share (5%) for FY26, subject to shareholder approval at the AGM. The record date for the dividend will be announced later. The board also approved the audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. Additionally, Ashika Stock Services Limited became a material subsidiary, and the in-principle approval for a proposed Mutual Fund sponsorship will be transferred to ASSL, pending SEBI approval. The statutory auditors, DHC & Co, resigned due to ineligibility as per RBI guidelines.

  • Ashika Credit Capital recommends a final dividend of ₹0.50 per share for FY26.
  • Audited financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026, were approved.
  • Ashika Stock Services Limited becomes a material wholly-owned subsidiary.
  • Transfer of Mutual Fund sponsorship approval to Ashika Stock Services Limited is underway.
  • Statutory auditors, DHC & Co, resigned due to RBI ineligibility criteria.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ashika Credit Capital Ltd | Category: Dividend

item.title: [ASHIKA] Ashika Credit Capital: FY26 Audited Results; Dividend of ₹0.50
item.description:
Ashika Credit Capital Ltd approved audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹0.50 per equity share. Ashika Stock Services Limited became a material subsidiary. The board also approved the transfer of the in-principle approval for sponsorship of the proposed Mutual Fund to Ashika Stock Services Limited, subject to SEBI approval. Statutory auditors M/s DHC & Co resigned due to ineligibility as per RBI guidelines.

  • Audited standalone and consolidated financial results approved for FY26.
  • Final dividend of ₹0.50 per share recommended.
  • Ashika Stock Services Limited becomes a material subsidiary.
  • Transfer of Mutual Fund sponsorship approval to Ashika Stock Services Ltd, pending SEBI approval.
  • Statutory auditors M/s DHC & Co resigned due to RBI ineligibility.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ashika Credit Capital Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [ASHIKA] Ashika Credit Capital: Deviation Statement for Mar 31, 2026
item.description:
Ashika Credit Capital Ltd submitted a statement of deviation for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026, in compliance with SEBI regulations. The company raised funds through the issue of equity convertible warrants on a preferential basis, but there were no warrant subscriptions received during the quarter. Therefore, funds could not be utilized towards the stated objects. Acuite Ratings & Research Limited is the monitoring agency. There was a deviation/variation in the use of funds raised.

  • Ashika Credit Capital reports a statement of deviation for the quarter ended March 31, 2026.
  • No warrant subscriptions were received during the quarter ending March 2026.
  • Original allocation for investment in shares & securities was ₹39.62 Cr, funds utilized were ₹2.41 Cr.
  • Original allocation for loan to Ashika Stock Broking Limited was ₹50.00 Cr, funds utilized were ₹25.00 Cr.
  • Total funds utilized were ₹27.41 Cr against an original allocation of ₹109.62 Cr.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ashika Credit Capital Ltd | Category: Quarterly Updates

item.title: [ASHIKA] Ashika Credit Capital: DHC & Co Resigns as Statutory Auditors
item.description:
Ashika Credit Capital Ltd (ACCL) announced the resignation of its statutory auditors, M/s DHC & Co, Chartered Accountants, effective May 17, 2026, due to ineligibility as per RBI guidelines after crossing the asset size threshold. The Board of Directors approved the audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026, with an unmodified opinion from DHC & Co. A final dividend of ₹0.50 per equity share (5%) was recommended, subject to shareholder approval. Ashika Stock Services Limited became a material wholly-owned subsidiary. The in-principle approval for sponsorship of the proposed Mutual Fund will be transferred to Ashika Stock Services Limited, pending SEBI approval.

  • DHC & Co resigns as statutory auditors due to RBI ineligibility criteria.
  • Audited financial results for FY26 approved with an unmodified opinion.
  • Final dividend of ₹0.50 per share recommended for FY26.
  • Ashika Stock Services Limited becomes a material wholly-owned subsidiary.
  • Transfer of Mutual Fund sponsorship approval to Ashika Stock Services Limited is underway.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ashika Credit Capital Ltd | Category: Auditor

item.title: [BSELINFRA] BSEL Algo Board Meeting on May 28, 2026 to Approve Audited Results
item.description:
BSEL Algo Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on May 28, 2026, to consider and approve the Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results for the financial year ended March 31, 2026. The trading window for designated persons and their immediate relatives has been closed from April 01, 2026, until 48 hours after the declaration of the results.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 28, 2026, to approve audited financial results.
  • Consideration of Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results for FY26.
  • Trading window closed from April 01, 2026, until 48 hours post results declaration.
  • Closure impacts designated persons and their immediate relatives.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BSEL INFRASTRUCTURE REALTY LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [ASHIKA] Ashika Credit Capital: Dividend of ₹0.50 Recommended
item.description:
Ashika Credit Capital Ltd's board approved audited financial results for the year ended March 31, 2026, recommending a final dividend of ₹0.50 per share (5%). Ashika Stock Services Limited became a material subsidiary. The board also approved the transfer of the in-principle approval for sponsorship of the proposed Mutual Fund to Ashika Stock Services Limited, subject to SEBI approval. Statutory auditors, M/s DHC & Co, resigned due to ineligibility as per RBI guidelines.

  • Final dividend of ₹0.50 per share (5%) recommended for FY26, subject to shareholder approval.
  • Ashika Stock Services Limited becomes a material wholly-owned subsidiary.
  • In-principle approval for Mutual Fund sponsorship to be transferred to Ashika Stock Services Limited, pending SEBI approval.
  • M/s DHC & Co resigns as statutory auditors due to RBI ineligibility guidelines.
  • Audited standalone and consolidated financial results for FY26 approved.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ashika Credit Capital Ltd | Category: Dividend
